Notice Hearing Setting On Plaintiff And Cross Defendant Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Oct-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Lauren E. Jones (707-509-9882; ljones@kdvlaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Scott Freedman (scott@zfplaw.com) for Defendant/cross-complainant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SUSAN MITCHELL's Motion For New Trial is denied. Mitchell contends that the court erred and abused its discretion by not continuing the summary adjudication motion for further discovery. Mitchell's request for a continuance, which was not supported by an affidavit in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(h), was untimely as it was first orally requested at the summary adjudication hearing. (See Roman v. BRE Properties, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1040, 1056 [request for continuance under section 437c(h) to be made in opposition to summary judgment motion or separate application no later than when opposition due].) Moreover, Mitchell was not diligent. Mitchell contends that her deposition is new evidence that warrants a new trial. Mitchell fails to explain why the information unearthed at her deposition could not have been presented in her opposing declaration. The court further notes that the main issue in the summary adjudication motion-whether the premises were exempt from the rent ordinance-was a legal issue based on undisputed facts and further discovery would not have altered the ruling.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
